name: Lago
what: Open-source billing, metering, subscriptions, invoicing, and usage-based pricing platform.
solves: Usage metering, recurring billing logic, invoices, credits, pricing plans, and finance-facing billing operations that are cumbersome to build from scratch.
users: SaaS/product operators and finance/ops teams that need sophisticated billing beyond direct Stripe checkout.
fit: Potentially useful if Hylono introduces memberships, device access plans, protocol subscriptions, or usage-based/rental billing complexity. For straightforward Stripe commerce it may be heavier than needed.
runtime: Web/API application with billing engine, workers, database, and connectors.
components: API/UI, billing engine, background jobs, PostgreSQL, message/queue layer depending on deployment, and external payment/invoicing integrations.
deploy: Official Docker Compose and Helm paths exist. Production requires persistent database, background processing, mail/notifications, and finance-grade change control.
deps: PostgreSQL, background workers/queues, email/notifications, webhooks, and payment provider integration.
auth: Built-in auth plus API keys and finance/admin roles.
apis: REST APIs, webhooks, billing events, metering ingestion, invoice/subscription management.
security: Billing systems directly affect revenue and customer trust. Webhook integrity, tax/invoice correctness, and finance-process controls matter.
setup_path: Start with official self-host docs and Docker Compose only after a billing domain model is agreed. Do not deploy speculatively.
env: DB URL, app secrets, payment provider credentials, webhooks, mailer config, storage if needed, and background job settings.
persistence: Back up the billing DB and preserve invoice artifacts. Test webhook replay and invoice regeneration assumptions carefully.
operations: Version upgrades require billing regression checks, webhook validation, and finance-side acceptance criteria.
failure_points: Overlapping responsibilities with Stripe, introducing invoice truth conflicts, and deploying metering before the business pricing model is stable.
integration: Could connect to Stripe, Next.js checkout/rental APIs, n8n automation, Twenty CRM account data, Novu notifications, and Metabase finance reporting.
verify: Define whether Hylono actually needs usage/subscription billing beyond current Stripe capabilities. Decide whether Lago or Invoice Ninja should own invoice generation if both are present.
risk: High domain-model risk if introduced too early.
gdpr: Contains customer billing data; treat as financial system of record if adopted.
overlap: Overlaps partly with Stripe Billing and Invoice Ninja/Akaunting depending on scope.
codex_read_first: Read current Hylono pricing, rental, and checkout flows before proposing Lago implementation.
codex_best_path: Use Lago only when subscription/usage billing is a confirmed requirement; isolate it as a dedicated billing service, not a casual add-on.
codex_never_assume: Never assume finance will accept a new billing source of truth without reconciliation, tax, and invoice workflow validation.
